Maternal Child Adolescent Health
Maternal Child and Adolescent Health (MCAH) is a part of the California Department of Public Health (CDPH). The Santa Barbara County Public Health Department (SBC PHD) administers the MCAH Program for Santa Barbara County residents. The MCAH program has the following goals:
Promote the well-being of individuals of reproductive age before, during, and after their pregnancy journey.
Ensure all infants are born healthy and thrive in their first year of life.
Decrease infant mortality rates, with an emphasis on eliminating disparities.
Optimize the healthy growth and development of all children to enable them to excel and reach their full potential.
Ensure that every individual of reproductive age, child, and their families has fair and unrestricted access to the necessary healthcare services through a well-coordinated and smooth healthcare system.
Make systems of care easier to navigate and increase engagement for children and youth with special health care needs and their families.
Enhance strengths, skills, and supports to promote positive development and ensure youth are healthy and thrive.
The SBC PHD Community Health Division's MCAH program includes a home visiting program staffed by Public Health Nurses and Health Educator Assistant Seniors (HEA, Sr.) dedicated to addressing the health disparities and health outcomes of families in Santa Barbara County. The program works to achieve optimal health for this population through case management and coordination of services. The goal is to enhance access to comprehensive quality healthcare and address factors that impact social determinants of health, including, but not limited to, housing, food, and transportation needs. The program is staffed by an MCAH Director who also serves as the program manager, an MCAH Coordinator, a Perinatal Services Coordinator (PSC), Public Health Nurses (PHNs), and Health Education Assistant Seniors (HEA, Sr.).
MCAH Home Visiting Services
MCAH Field Nursing Unit
Public Health Nurses (PHNs) provide in-home assessment, education, linkage/referral, and comprehensive case management for pregnant or postpartum individuals, infants, children, and adolescents at high risk for adverse prenatal outcomes, maltreatment of children, substance abuse, postpartum depression, medical fragility, and childhood obesity. All pregnant and postpartum individuals, infants, children, and teens are evaluated for needed access to health care. Pregnant and postpartum individuals with prenatal services at the SB County Clinics will receive a CPSP (Comprehensive Perinatal Services Program) postpartum visit. Contact: 800-288-8145
MCAH Program Brochure (PDF)
Healthy Families America ®
Healthy Families America
® is a nationally recognized program that connects expectant parents and parents of newborns with free child development assistance in their homes. Home visitors provide guidance, information, and support to help parents be the best parents they can be.  The mission of Healthy Families is to promote supportive environments that optimize child growth and development and encourage resilient, healthy families. Healthy Families Santa Barbara County is administered by the Santa Barbara County Public Health Department's Maternal, Child & Adolescent Health Program.
HFA Program Brochure (PDF)
Home Visiting Services Referral Form (PDF)
MCAH Services and Programs
Maternal Child & Adolescent Health Strategic Planning
The MCAH program is responsible for planning and guiding the Scope of Work of the local MCAH program. A Community Health Assessment is completed every five years to understand the health and well-being of the population we serve and use the results to plan local programs. Nineteen focus areas of MCAH are used to determine service level, unmet needs, outcome evaluation, and program capacity. Collaborative participation is key in addressing the needs of the MCAH Population. MCAH Director and staff participate in over twelve Santa Barbara collaboratives, such as the Oral Health Executive Team, Safe Kids Network, Child Abuse Prevention Council, Breastfeeding Coalition, and THRIVE. Contact: 800-288-8145
Perinatal Services
Community-based system of comprehensive perinatal care for pregnant and postpartum individuals, including prenatal, delivery, postpartum, neonatal, and infant care, to meet each individual's unique needs. These services help support a healthy pregnancy and delivery, reducing maternal and infant mortality and morbidity. We collaborate, support, and bring programs to the community that encourage ongoing education, self-sufficiency, healthy life choices, and mental and physical wellness during pregnancy.
https://www.cdph.ca.gov/programs/cfh/dmcah/cpsp/pages/default.aspx
Sudden Infant Death Syndrome Program
A Public Health Nurse will conduct a home visit on any suspected sudden infant death syndrome (SIDS) death to provide information and/or support for the parents, caregiver, or family. We actively collaborate with community providers to deliver SAFE Sleep and SIDS Risk Reduction education. We strive to empower families with the knowledge and tools necessary to create safe sleep environments for infants, thereby working towards preventing SIDS.
Contact: 800-288-8145.
Child Death Review Team
The Child Death Review Team (CDRT) comprises multidisciplinary team members from Santa Barbara County. The team's purpose is to conduct in-depth reviews of preventable child deaths, identify system gaps, and develop preventive strategies. There is an emphasis on abuse and neglect reviews. Contact: 805-681-4933
